title: Evangelion tin box or the individual platinum DVDs
display_title: Evangelion tin box or the individual platinum DVDs
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion tin box or the individual platinum DVDs             Quote Post by TheSophist » Tue May 18, 2010 12:29 am just wondering if the tin box has something more than the DVDs bought individally? http://hmv.com/hmvweb/simpleMultiSearch.do?searchUID=8360633715752912113&pGroupID=0&adultFlag=false&primaryID=0&simpleSearchString=evangelion+platinum or http://www.amazon.co.uk/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Ultimate-Collection/dp/B000SKKCPA/ref=sr_1_3?ie=UTF8&s=dvd&qid=1274160194&sr=1-3

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue May 18, 2010 12:51 am the tin box & the individual DVDs are basically the same product, with all the same versions of the episodes, all the same extras, etc. The tin box obviously has a tin box that didn't come with the individual DVDs, but it also lacks all the inserts that come with the individuals. That stuff is available online, though. Tin box thinpack set (called the Perfect Platinum) is also cheaper. EDIT oh wait looks like that release comes with a 60-page booklet, so you even get fancy written material with your thinpacks. That's cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Shazlicks » Tue Jun 08, 2010 5:08 am I lent my platinum box set to someone and never got it back. i have looked around and have had no success, someone here might be able to enlighten me. is the 60page booklet with the tin, the same as the individual booklets you get with the single dvd's?

[]: Re: Evangelion tin box or the individual platinum DVDs             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 08, 2010 7:32 am TheSophist wrote: just wondering if the tin box has something more than the DVDs bought individally?
